summary: Comprehensive regression testing of Agent Configuration Manager (ACM) after ChatWizard.js refactoring completed successfully. Both critical fixes verified: 1) MCP tools and subagent options properly integrated in Chat Wizard with extensive step definitions (lines 157-237), 2) YAML output scrollability implemented with overflow-y-auto max-h-[400px] container (lines 674-676). Backend maintains 100% API functionality. All core features including Agent List, Agent Editor, Version History, Clone, Compare View, and Chat Wizard are working correctly with no regressions detected.
passed_tests: Backend API maintains 100% functionality (10/10 tests passed)
Agent List displays 18 agents correctly with proper layout
Search functionality working perfectly (found 2 E2 agents on search)
Agent Editor accessible via agent name links
Chat Wizard accessible at /wizard with conversational interface
Chat Wizard step counter showing proper progression (1/14 format)
Navigation links working (Agents, Wizard, Compare)
CRITICAL FIX #1 VERIFIED: MCP tools and subagent steps in ChatWizard.js
MCP builtin tools step with multi-select functionality (lines 149-156)
MCP toolset addition step with yes/no choice (lines 158-167)
MCP configuration steps (name, url, transport, tools) (lines 169-204)
Subagent addition step with configuration options (lines 206-237)
CRITICAL FIX #2 VERIFIED: YAML scrollability with overflow-y-auto max-h-[400px]
YAML output container changed from ScrollArea to plain div (lines 674-676)
Agent Editor tabs navigation working
Save and Export buttons present in Agent Editor
Version History accessible via History button
Clone functionality accessible from agent actions
Compare View accessible via navigation
All core CRUD operations working via backend API
Provider filtering and search controls present
